component.name: Dofek
component.summary: AI-aware system monitor with a trading-terminal interface

description.p: Dofek is a system monitor for developers who run AI workloads on their workstation. A single Rust codebase produces both a terminal TUI and a Tauri-based desktop GUI; they share the same data layer.

ul.li: CPU load, per-core, temperature, and (Intel/RAPL) power.
GPU utilization, VRAM, temperature, and power — NVIDIA via NVML, with a LibreHardwareMonitor fallback on Windows for non-NVIDIA cards.
Per-process VRAM accounting, plus AI-workload detection (Loading / Inferring / Idle states).
Memory, swap, network rates, and disk I/O (cross-platform).
System-tray companion with a live CPU sparkline icon, close-to-tray, and macOS menu-bar text.
Linux CPU power via RAPL (closes a long-standing N/A on Linux).
Cross-platform disk I/O chart and ticker pill.

description.p: Extensible via a JSON-over-stdio plugin protocol; ships with Ollama and Docker plugins that pin matching processes in the watchlist.

screenshot.caption: Trading-terminal GUI: CPU candlestick, GPU utilization, watchlist with AI-workload badges
Terminal TUI: same data, half-block charts, keyboard-driven

description.p: UX polish on the v1.3 tray companion. Tray "Settings" menu item now opens the help/settings overlay (was a silent no-op). TUI prints a clear warning on startup when the terminal lacks truecolor support — Apple Terminal.app is the most common case; iTerm2 / WezTerm / Ghostty / Alacritty / Kitty all render correctly. Release pipeline no longer reverts its own releases to draft on retag.

System-tray companion (live CPU sparkline icon, close-to-tray default, right-click Show/Hide/Settings/Quit, macOS menu-bar text). Linux CPU power via RAPL. Cross-platform disk I/O metrics with new DISK chart tab and ticker pill. macOS Apple Silicon now ships as a .dmg in addition to .app.zip.
macOS (Apple Silicon) support — TUI and GUI on aarch64-apple-darwin, three-platform release pipeline.
Linux x86_64 support — TUI and GUI, native .deb / .rpm / .AppImage bundles.
First public release: MSI installer, dofek.dev, plugin protocol stabilized, GitHub Actions release pipeline.
